notes: Day 27 (Mar 26) UPDATE 4PM ET. Sources: CNN D27 live, AJ liveblog D27, NPR, CNBC, AP, Bloomberg, Fortune, The National, Times of Israel, Investing.com, Euronews, Iran International, JPost, Fox News, PBS, WSJ, Haaretz, Israel Hayom, ToI, ABC News, WashPost, Al-Monitor, SCMP, Kurdistan24, Sunday Guardian, Pew Research, HRANA, Hengaw, Wikipedia, IndiaTV, TheWeek India, Insurance Journal.

TANGSIRI KILLED — IRGC NAVY COMMANDER: Israel confirmed killing Alireza Tangsiri, IRGC Navy Commander since 2018, in strike on Bandar Abbas. Defense Minister Katz confirmed. Tangsiri ARCHITECTED Hormuz blockade, commanded 1st Naval District. Born Bushehr, Iran-Iraq War veteran. KEY figure behind Hormuz closure + safe passage framework. Death disrupts IRGC naval command structure.

ISRAEL SPEEDING UP STRIKES — 48-HR TARGETING BLITZ: IDF completed 'wave of extensive strikes in Isfahan targeting infrastructure.' NPR: military officials say IDF speeding up targeting over next 48 hours, hitting arms factories 'in case ceasefire is declared.' Creating fait accompli before potential deal. Ambassador Danon: Israel NOT part of US-Iran talks, ops continue until nuclear/missile eliminated.

IRAN 6 MISSILE SALVOS AT ISRAEL: Iran fired 6 missile salvos at central Israel within hours. Bnei Brak apartment hit (2 injured). Kfar Qassem struck (Mar 26). Tel Aviv shrapnel (3 wounded — possible cluster munition). Hezbollah drone downed over Galilee (2 lightly hurt). Total D27 Israel injuries: 9+.

IRAN FORMALLY REJECTED 15-POINT PLAN — 5 COUNTER-CONDITIONS: FM Araghchi: 'no negotiations,' 'war ends on our terms.' 5 conditions: (1) end aggression, (2) guarantees no recurrence, (3) reparations, (4) comprehensive end ALL fronts incl Lebanon, (5) sovereignty over Hormuz. Called US plan 'maximalist and unreasonable.' US official: Iran conditions 'ridiculous and unrealistic' (WSJ).

TRUMP: 4 MORE DAYS — FRIDAY DEADLINE: Trump said Tehran has 4 more days (from Mar 25) to agree before US targets energy infrastructure. Friday Mar 28 = expiration of 5-day postponement from Mar 23. Day 4 of 5. White House: 'productive talks.' Pentagon: war 'ahead of schedule.'

OIL SURGED D27: Brent $106.12 (+3.8%, CNBC). WTI $93.61 (+3.6%, CNBC). Surged after Iran rejected direct talks. Previous day had dropped to $102.22. Market in backwardation — Dec futures $79.70 (17% below front-month). Iran Hormuz toll legislation being prepared.

CASUALTIES D27: Iran: 1,750+ killed (IRNA, up from 1,500+). HRANA: 1,455 civilian + 217 children + 1,167 military + 669 unclassified. Hengaw D25 report: 6,530 killed incl 640 civilians. Lebanon: 1,094 killed (up from 1,072), 121 children. Iraq: 96 killed. Israel: 18+. Gulf: 22+ dead. UAE national death toll: 11. IDF soldier killed Lebanon (Staff Sgt. Greenberg, Golani).

UAE D27: Ballistic missile intercepted over Abu Dhabi — debris killed 2, injured 3. National death toll now 11. UAE reports 2,100+ missiles/drones received total since D1. Sirens sounded Thursday morning.

SAFE PASSAGE EXPANDING: India declared 'Strait of Hormuz open for India.' Two Indian LPG tankers passed through. Thailand Bangchak tanker passed after talks with Iran. Iran preparing Hormuz toll legislation (Fars). Insurance premiums surged 5-10% of hull value (from 0.15-0.25% pre-war). 2,000 vessels / 20,000 seafarers stranded.

PEW POLL D27: 59% Americans say WRONG decision. 61% disapprove Trump handling. 54% expect 6+ months. 88% Dems oppose. 71% Reps support. AP-NORC: 59% gone too far. Quinnipiac: 54-39 oppose. House Armed Services Committee unsatisfied with briefing.
GCC JOINT STATEMENT: UAE, Qatar, Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan condemned Iran attacks. GCC SG: Iran 'overcome all red lines.' Germany defense minister: 'catastrophe for world's economies.'
8,000 TONS ARMS TO ISRAEL: 200+ aircraft and ships delivering since D1. US embassy Baghdad: advise Americans leave Iraq immediately.

sitrep: TANGSIRI KILLED — IRGC NAVY COMMANDER ELIMINATED: Israel confirmed killing Alireza Tangsiri, IRGC Navy Commander since 2018, in strike on Bandar Abbas. Defense Minister Katz confirmed. Tangsiri ARCHITECTED Hormuz blockade — commanded 1st Naval District, shaped Iran's maritime defense strategy. His death disrupts IRGC naval command structure at critical moment when safe passage framework is expanding. KEY QUESTION: Does Tangsiri's death weaken or harden Iran's Hormuz posture?

ISRAEL SPEEDING UP STRIKES — FAIT ACCOMPLI STRATEGY: IDF completed 'wave of extensive strikes in Isfahan targeting infrastructure.' NPR: military officials say IDF speeding up targeting arms factories over next 48 hours 'in case ceasefire is declared.' CLASSIC FAIT ACCOMPLI — destroy as much as possible before political deal. Ambassador Danon: Israel NOT part of US-Iran talks, military ops continue until nuclear/missile capabilities eliminated. US-Israel divergence deepening.

IRAN FORMALLY REJECTED 15-POINT PLAN — 5 COUNTER-CONDITIONS: FM Araghchi: 'no negotiations, war ends on our terms.' Five conditions: (1) end aggression/assassinations, (2) mechanisms guaranteeing no recurrence, (3) war reparations, (4) comprehensive end ALL fronts incl Lebanon/Iraq, (5) international recognition of Iranian sovereignty over Hormuz. Called US plan 'maximalist and unreasonable.' Iranian security official: conditions 'excessive and detached from US failure on battlefield.' US official: Iran conditions 'ridiculous and unrealistic' (WSJ). GAP IS ENORMOUS.

IRAN 6 MISSILE SALVOS AT ISRAEL — SUSTAINED FIRE: 6 missile salvos at central Israel within hours. Bnei Brak apartment hit (2 injured). Kfar Qassem projectile strike (Mar 26). Tel Aviv shrapnel wounds (3 injured — possible cluster munition). Hezbollah drone downed Galilee (2 lightly hurt). Despite Tangsiri death and 27 days of bombing, Iran STILL launching multi-salvo attacks at Israel's heartland.

TRUMP: 4 MORE DAYS — FRIDAY DEADLINE APPROACHING: Trump said Tehran has 4 more days to agree before US targets energy infrastructure. Day 4 of 5-day postponement (from Mar 23). Friday Mar 28 = deadline. White House: 'productive talks,' war 'ahead of schedule.' Pentagon deploying 82nd Airborne. Diplomatic window closing.

OIL SURGED D27: Brent $106.12 (+3.8%, CNBC). WTI $93.61 (+3.6%). Surged after Iran rejected talks. Market in backwardation (Dec $79.70 = 17% below front-month). Iran preparing Hormuz toll legislation. Insurance 5-10% hull value. Oil whipsawing daily on diplomacy signals.

SAFE PASSAGE EXPANDING DESPITE TANGSIRI DEATH: India declared 'Strait of Hormuz open for India' — two LPG tankers passed. Thailand Bangchak tanker passed after talks with Iran. Safe passage framework expanding even as architect killed. Iran preparing toll legislation. 2,000 vessels / 20,000 seafarers still stranded. Legal experts: Iran has no legal basis for controlling passage.

CASUALTIES D27: Iran: 1,750+ killed (IRNA, up from 1,500+). Hengaw: 6,530 killed. HRANA: 3,291+ tracked. Lebanon: 1,094 killed (up from 1,072), 121 children. Iraq: 96 killed. Israel: 18+. Gulf: 22+. UAE: 11 national dead. IDF soldier killed Lebanon (Golani). 82,000+ structures.

PEW POLL — 59% WRONG DECISION: 61% disapprove Trump handling. 54% expect 6+ months more war. 88% Democrats oppose. 71% Republicans support. AP-NORC: 59% gone too far. House Armed Services Committee unsatisfied with briefing. Political pressure mounting.

GCC JOINT CONDEMNATION: UAE, Qatar, Saudi, Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan joint statement condemning Iran. GCC SG: 'overcome all red lines.' Germany defense minister: 'catastrophe for world's economies.' 8,000 tons arms delivered to Israel (200+ aircraft/ships).

theoryEvaluation.title: TANGSIRI DEATH — does Hormuz blockade survive its architect?
theoryEvaluation.body: Tangsiri commanded IRGC Navy since 2018, ran 1st Naval District from Bandar Abbas, shaped Iran's entire maritime defense strategy and Hormuz blockade. His death is THE most significant kill for Hormuz operations. KEY QUESTION: Is the blockade institutional or personal? Evidence suggests INSTITUTIONAL — safe passage framework expanding (India, Thailand transited SAME DAY as death reported). Toll legislation being prepared = bureaucratic momentum. But naval command succession unclear. If successor is less competent: mine-clearing easier, safe passage accelerates. If IRGC hardliners promote aggressive replacement: could harden posture. MOST LIKELY: short-term disruption, medium-term institutional continuity.

theoryEvaluation.title: IRAN'S FORMAL REJECTION — negotiation posture or final answer?
theoryEvaluation.body: Iran formally rejected 15-point plan with 5 counter-conditions. FM Araghchi: 'no negotiations, war ends on our terms.' US called Iran conditions 'ridiculous.' KEY ANALYSIS: (1) Rejection is EXPECTED first move in any negotiation — maximalist opening position. (2) 5 conditions ARE structured = engaging with framework even while rejecting substance. (3) Condition 4 (all fronts incl Lebanon) reveals Iran's ACTUAL priority: protecting proxy network, not just own territory. (4) Condition 5 (Hormuz sovereignty) = Iran wants international RECOGNITION of Hormuz control, not just de facto. (5) Both sides calling each other 'unrealistic' = classic negotiation stance. CONCLUSION: This is negotiation posture, not final answer. But gap is enormous and Friday deadline creates pressure.

theoryEvaluation.title: ISRAEL FAIT ACCOMPLI — IDF racing to create irreversible facts
theoryEvaluation.body: IDF speeding up targeting arms factories 'in case ceasefire is declared' = CLASSIC fait accompli strategy. Ambassador Danon: Israel NOT part of US-Iran talks, ops continue until nuclear/missile eliminated. KEY IMPLICATIONS: (1) US-Israel DIVERGENCE deepening — Trump seeking deal, Israel destroying factories. (2) IDF timeline 'several more weeks' contradicts Trump's Friday deadline. (3) If ceasefire declared: Israel may not comply. (4) Arms factory destruction creates irreversible military facts regardless of diplomatic outcome. (5) Isfahan strikes = targeting Iran's ability to rebuild. THIS IS THE MOST DANGEROUS US-ISRAEL SPLIT since D1. Trump needs deal for political reasons (Pew 59%). Israel wants maximum destruction. These goals are increasingly incompatible.

theoryEvaluation.title: DOMESTIC PRESSURE — Pew 59% and political dynamics
theoryEvaluation.body: Pew: 59% wrong decision, 61% disapprove handling, 54% expect 6+ months. AP-NORC: 59% gone too far. House Armed Services unsatisfied. KEY: (1) These numbers are UNPRECEDENTED for a war only 27 days old. (2) Iraq War didn't reach these disapproval levels for MONTHS. (3) 88% Dem oppose vs 71% Rep support = deep partisan divide. (4) Republican-leaning independents SPLIT 52-45 = Trump's coalition fraying. (5) Oil prices + gas prices directly visible to voters. (6) This creates political incentive for Trump to seek quick exit — reinforcing declared victory scenario. (7) But also constrains escalation options (power plants). CONCLUSION: Pew numbers accelerate political timeline for deal/exit. Trump NEEDS off-ramp more than Iran does domestically.

theoryEvaluation_fa.body: تانگسیری از ۲۰۱۸ فرمانده نیروی دریایی. بندرعباس. استراتژی دریایی کل ایران. مهم‌ترین ضربه هرمز. سؤال: نهادی یا شخصی؟ شواهد: نهادی — عبور گسترش همان روز. عوارض = بروکراتیک. جانشینی نامشخص. کوتاه‌مدت اختلال.

endstate_fa.title: چهارم (۱۱٪ / ۱۰٪ / ۷٪)
endstate_fa.body: مداخله (۱۱٪) — GCC + آلمان + پیو + G7. فروپاشی (۱۰٪) — تانگسیری + مجتبی ۱۸. مذاکره (۷٪) — ایران رسماً رد. شکاف بزرگ.

shippingRoutes.detail: Permanently dead. Mines + Tangsiri's death doesn't change US exclusion. Iran's safe passage framework explicitly excludes US-flagged vessels. Toll legislation being prepared. Mine clearance weeks/months. Insurance 5-10% hull value.
shippingRoutes.eta: N/A. Bilateral routes for Pakistan/India/China/Thailand only

shippingRoutes.detail: Closed. NATO airspace restrictions. Turkey-Iran land border shut. Internet Day 27 (600+ hrs). No improvement signals.
shippingRoutes.eta: Not viable until conflict ends + diplomatic normalization

shippingRoutes.detail: Still most viable. Power plant threat postponed (Friday deadline). 82nd Airborne focus = Kharg not internal Iran. Internet Day 27. 82,000+ structures. Hospitals 1/3. Food tripled. Grid INTACT. Tangsiri death = Hormuz command disrupted, not land routes.
shippingRoutes.eta: If power grid survives: 4-8 weeks Tehran->Yerevan->sea. Viability stable

shippingRoutes.detail: UPGRADED: Pakistan active mediator + first Hormuz transit. Safe passage expanding. India declared 'open.' Thailand tanker passed. Eastern borders not in combat zone. Tangsiri death = potential disruption to safe passage coordination but framework appears institutional.
shippingRoutes.eta: Months to establish direct route. Safe passage framework = positive precedent

shippingRoutes.detail: Oman ports hit by IRGC drones. Mines. Safe passage framework could include Oman eventually. Tangsiri death = naval command disrupted. Monitor successor's posture.
shippingRoutes.eta: Uncertain — mines + safe passage + Tangsiri successor = competing dynamics

shippingRoutes_fa.detail: مرده. مین + تانگسیری مرده اما آمریکا خارج. عوارض. بیمه ۵-۱۰٪.
بهترین. نیروگاه تعویق (جمعه). هوابرد ۸۲ = خارگ نه داخل. اینترنت ۲۷. برق سالم. تانگسیری = دریایی نه زمینی.
پاکستان + هند + تایلند عبور. تانگسیری مرده اما نهادی. شرقی امن.
حمله شد. مین. تانگسیری = فرماندهی مختل. جانشین نظارت.

publishingStatus: Paper imports: HALTED since D1. Iran's paper supply is 70%+ imported via Hormuz. With mines deployed and Hormuz physically closed, no bulk paper shipments are entering the country. Pre-war warehouse stock is the only supply. Major publishers (Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz) rationing remaining inventory.

Printing capacity: Isfahan and Shiraz — Iran's two largest publishing centers after Tehran — are in active strike zones. Isfahan struck extensively D27 (IDF targeting arms factories in same industrial zones). Tehran printing houses operating intermittently due to power fluctuations and staff unable to commute.

Internet: 600+ hours of total blackout = no digital communications with authors, no email proofs, no online ordering systems, no digital pre-press workflows. Publishers cannot coordinate with distributors, typesetters, or translators. Pre-digital workflow (phone/fax) impossible — phone networks also degraded.

New title production: EFFECTIVELY HALTED since ~D5. No new ISBNs being processed (Ministry of Culture offices closed). No new manuscripts entering production pipeline. Only books already at printer before war could potentially be completed. Estimate: zero new titles until at minimum 3 months post-ceasefire.

Warehouse stock: This is what Iranfarhang can sell. Pre-war inventory in Tehran warehouses is the ONLY available product. Unknown condition — 82,000+ structures destroyed, no way to verify warehouse integrity without internet/phone. If Tehran warehouse intact: existing catalog fulfillable once a route reopens. If damaged: total loss.

Distribution: Domestic distribution network (bookstores, universities) collapsed. Transportation within Iran dangerous — 700km from Tehran to any border crosses active strike zones. Fuel available but convoys risky.
publishingStatus_fa: واردات کاغذ: متوقف از روز ۱. ۷۰٪+ از هرمز. فقط انبار قبل جنگ. جیره‌بندی.
چاپخانه: اصفهان و شیراز در منطقه حمله. اصفهان D27 گسترده. تهران متناوب.
اینترنت: ۶۰۰+ ساعت = ارتباط صفر. ایمیل، سفارش، پیش‌چاپ، هماهنگی ناممکن.
تولید جدید: متوقف از ~D5. ISBN صادر نمی‌شود. وزارت تعطیل. حداقل ۳ ماه بعد آتش‌بس.
انبار: تنها محصول موجود. وضعیت نامعلوم — اینترنت/تلفن نیست. اگر سالم: کاتالوگ موجود. اگر آسیب: ضرر کامل.
توزیع: شبکه داخلی فروپاشیده. ۷۰۰ کیلومتر تا مرز از منطقه حمله عبور می‌کند.
impactIranfarhang: SUPPLY CHAIN: 100% SEVERED. Zero books have left Iran since D1. Cannot ship, cannot communicate with Tehran office, cannot verify warehouse inventory. 27 consecutive days of total operational blackout. This is not a disruption — it is a complete cessation of the supply pipeline.

Revenue impact: No new inventory = fulfilling ONLY from Atlanta office pre-war stock (whatever was already in-country). Once that runs out, revenue drops to zero until a route reopens. Estimate: 2-4 months of existing US-side inventory depending on order volume. University library ordering cycles (fall semester) begin June-July — if no route by then, miss entire FY27 cycle.

Route status (see shipping routes below): Dubai route = permanently dead for US-linked shipments. Armenia = only viable path (~10-15%) but requires 700km overland through active strike zones with no internet to coordinate. CRITICAL DEPENDENCY: Iran's power grid must survive Friday deadline — if power plants struck, Armenia route drops to <5%.

OFAC exemption: 31 CFR §560.210(c) informational materials exemption is STATUTORY (Berman Amendment). It survives any sanctions escalation — this is not an executive order that can be revoked. Legal right to import is intact. The problem is purely logistical, not legal.

KEMCO payments: France-based payment intermediary should be unaffected by conflict directly. However, if US imposes new secondary sanctions on Iran-linked financial flows, KEMCO's banking relationships could face pressure. Monitor US Treasury OFAC SDN updates.

Customer communications: Brief university library clients NOW: indefinite supply disruption, fulfill from existing US stock only, no timeline for new titles. Academic libraries plan 6-12 months ahead — the sooner they know, the better the relationship survives.

Family in Tehran: Internet 600+ hours. Cannot verify safety or operational status. 82,000+ structures destroyed. Food prices tripled. Hospitals at 1/3 capacity. No reliable way to communicate until internet restored or someone physically crosses a border.
impactIranfarhang_fa: زنجیره تأمین: ۱۰۰٪ قطع. هیچ کتابی از روز ۱ خارج نشده. ارسال، ارتباط، تأیید انبار — همه صفر. ۲۷ روز توقف کامل.
درآمد: فقط موجودی دفتر آتلانتا. ۲-۴ ماه. اگر مسیر تا ژوئن باز نشود: سیکل FY27 از دست می‌رود.
مسیرها: دبی = مرده برای آمریکا. ارمنستان = تنها (~۱۰-۱۵٪) اما ۷۰۰ کیلومتر. بقای شبکه برق حیاتی — جمعه.
OFAC: معافیت قانونی (برمن). ابطال‌ناپذیر. مشکل لجستیکی نه قانونی.
پرداخت KEMCO: فرانسه. فعلاً سالم. تحریم‌های ثانویه جدید = ریسک بانکی. SDN نظارت.
مشتریان: کتابخانه‌های دانشگاهی اطلاع دهید: اختلال نامحدود. فقط موجودی آمریکا. زمان‌بندی ندارد.
خانواده تهران: اینترنت ۶۰۰+. تأیید ایمنی ناممکن. ۸۲,۰۰۰+ سازه. غذا سه‌برابر.
prepIranfarhang: IMMEDIATE — Inventory audit: Count every book in Atlanta office. Catalog by subject, condition, and demand. This is your ONLY sellable stock for months. Prioritize high-demand academic titles for active university orders.

IMMEDIATE — Client outreach: Email all university library acquisition departments: 'Supply disruption due to conflict in Iran. Fulfilling from existing US inventory. New titles unavailable until further notice. We will update monthly.' Protect the 25-year relationships — transparency now prevents lost accounts later.

THIS WEEK — Armenia route planning: If power grid survives Friday, begin pre-negotiating: Tehran warehouse → truck to Agarak/Norduz border (700km) → Armenia → Yerevan → Poti/Batumi (Georgia, Black Sea) → container ship → US East Coast. Need: (1) Iranian trucker willing to drive through strike zones, (2) Armenian freight forwarder, (3) Georgian port agent. Cannot coordinate any of this until internet restores or a family member reaches a border with phone access.

THIS WEEK — KEMCO check: Contact KEMCO SARL in France. Verify banking relationships intact. Ask about any compliance concerns with French/EU banks regarding Iran-linked transactions during active conflict. France has been vocal ('catastrophe') — unlikely to tighten, but verify.

FRIDAY MAR 28 — WATCH: If Trump deadline passes with no deal and power plants are struck: (1) Armenia route drops to <5% viability (no electricity = no warehouses, no logistics), (2) Recovery timeline extends from months to years, (3) Entire pre-war inventory in Iran potentially inaccessible. This is the single biggest threat to the business.

MEDIUM-TERM — Alternative sourcing: Are there any Persian-language publishers outside Iran? Diaspora publishers in US, Canada, Europe, or Tajikistan? Could fill gaps for some academic categories. Not a replacement but a supplement.

MEDIUM-TERM — Digital pivot: University libraries increasingly accept digital. If any Iranian publishers had pre-war digital files/PDFs: could you license and distribute digitally under OFAC exemption? Explore with existing publisher relationships (once communications restore).
prepIranfarhang_fa: فوری — حسابرسی موجودی: شمارش کتاب‌های آتلانتا. دسته‌بندی. این تنها محصول فروشی است.
فوری — ارتباط مشتری: ایمیل به کتابخانه‌ها: اختلال نامحدود. موجودی آمریکا. به‌روزرسانی ماهانه. روابط ۲۵ ساله حفظ.
این هفته — ارمنستان: اگر برق سالم: تهران → آگاراک → ایروان → پوتی → کشتی → آمریکا. نیاز: راننده ایرانی + باربری ارمنی + نماینده گرجستان. بدون اینترنت هماهنگی ناممکن.
این هفته — KEMCO: تماس فرانسه. بانک سالم؟ تحریم‌های ثانویه؟ فرانسه 'فاجعه' گفته = احتمال سخت‌گیری کم.
جمعه ۲۸: اگر نیروگاه: ارمنستان <۵٪. بازیابی سال‌ها. انبار ایران غیرقابل دسترس. بزرگ‌ترین تهدید.
میان‌مدت — منابع جایگزین: ناشران فارسی خارج ایران؟ دیاسپورا آمریکا/کانادا/اروپا/تاجیکستان؟ مکمل نه جایگزین.
میان‌مدت — دیجیتال: کتابخانه‌ها دیجیتال قبول می‌کنند. فایل‌های قبل جنگ؟ مجوز و توزیع دیجیتال تحت OFAC؟
signalsIranfarhang: GATE 1 — POWER GRID (Friday Mar 28): If power plants struck → ALL Iran logistics collapse. Armenia route drops to <5%. Warehouse operations impossible. This single event determines whether recovery takes months or years. HIGHEST PRIORITY signal.

GATE 2 — INTERNET RESTORATION: Cannot do ANYTHING until communications restore. No warehouse verification, no route coordination, no family contact. Watch for: partial restoration, VPN availability, satellite internet (Starlink unlikely in Iran). Even SMS/phone restoration would help.

GATE 3 — CEASEFIRE / DEAL: Pew 59% + Friday deadline + Iran counter-conditions = political dynamics favor eventual exit. A ceasefire doesn't reopen routes immediately but starts the clock on recovery. Watch for: Trump 'declared victory,' partial ceasefire, Hormuz-specific agreement separate from nuclear deal.

ROUTE — Armenia border status: US Embassy Yerevan daily alerts confirm Agarak/Norduz border open. Monitor for: border closures, increased security checks, Iranian side closures, road conditions through strike zones. This is the only viable land route.

ROUTE — Safe passage expansion: India, Thailand, Pakistan transiting Hormuz. If framework expands to commercial goods (not just oil): potential future route for book shipments via third-country flag vessel. HOWEVER: Iran's framework explicitly excludes US-linked — Iranfarhang shipments to Atlanta would likely not qualify.
FINANCIAL — OFAC SDN updates: Monitor US Treasury for new designations that could affect KEMCO or French banking. Low probability but high impact.

INVENTORY — Tehran warehouse: Any information about physical conditions in Tehran warehouse district. Satellite imagery, social media from Iran (rare given blackout), reports from diaspora contacts who may have phone/SMS contact.
signalsIranfarhang_fa: دروازه ۱ — برق (جمعه ۲۸): نیروگاه = فروپاشی لجستیک. ارمنستان <۵٪. بازیابی سال‌ها. بالاترین اولویت.
دروازه ۲ — اینترنت: بدون ارتباط هیچ کاری ممکن نیست. انبار، مسیر، خانواده. VPN، ماهواره، حتی SMS.
دروازه ۳ — آتش‌بس: پیو ۵۹٪ + جمعه + ضدشرط = خروج سیاسی. آتش‌بس ≠ بازگشایی فوری اما شروع بازیابی.
مسیر — ارمنستان: سفارت ایروان: مرز باز. نظارت: بسته شدن، بازرسی، جاده.
مسیر — عبور مطمئن: اگر کالای تجاری = آینده. اما آمریکایی خارج = ایرانفرهنگ احتمالاً واجد شرایط نیست.
مالی — OFAC SDN: تحریم‌های جدید = KEMCO/بانک فرانسه. احتمال کم اما تأثیر بالا.
انبار — تهران: هر اطلاعاتی از وضعیت فیزیکی. ماهواره‌ای، شبکه‌های اجتماعی، دیاسپورا.
impactKIP: DUBAI HUB: OPERATIONALLY DEAD. The Iran-UAE trade corridor — KIP's entire logistics backbone — has been non-functional since D1. UAE airspace repeatedly closed. Abu Dhabi hit by ballistic missile debris D27 (2 killed, national total 11). UAE has received 2,100+ projectiles. IRGC declared UAE bases 'legitimate targets.' Dubai re-export model is offline indefinitely.

Banking: EXTREME FREEZE RISK. UAE banks are under intense scrutiny for Iran-linked transactions. Any accounts, trade finance facilities, or letters of credit with Iranian beneficial ownership face potential freeze WITHOUT notice. UAE Central Bank compliance teams are in crisis mode — they will err on the side of freezing. If KIP has ANY funds in UAE banking system: withdrawal risk is CRITICAL.

Hormuz: PHYSICALLY BLOCKED. Mines confirmed (Maham 3/7). Even with safe passage framework expanding (India, Thailand, Pakistan), this is for OIL TANKERS under bilateral agreements — not general commercial cargo. KIP's import-export goods would need to transit on vessels with specific Iranian coordination, which is not available for commercial re-export to/from Dubai.

Personnel safety: 11 dead in UAE from conflict. 2,100+ projectiles received. Debris killing civilians in Abu Dhabi. If KIP has staff in Dubai/Abu Dhabi: temporary relocation to Oman (Muscat) or further is strongly recommended. Insurance for UAE-based operations has become prohibitively expensive or unavailable.

Inventory in transit / warehoused in Dubai: Any goods sitting in Jebel Ali Free Zone, Dubai warehouses, or in-transit via Hormuz are stranded. 2,000 vessels / 20,000 seafarers stuck in the region. Insurance 5-10% hull value (from 0.15-0.25% pre-war). Goods may be physically safe in Dubai warehouses but cannot move in or out via Hormuz.

Revenue: NEAR-ZERO from Iran corridor. No goods flowing in either direction. Iran side: internet 600+ hours blackout = cannot coordinate. Dubai side: logistics frozen, banking risky. Revenue from Iran-UAE trade is zero until both Hormuz reopens AND UAE airspace/banking normalizes. Timeline: months minimum, potentially 12-18 months for full corridor restoration.

Sanctions risk: US may impose new secondary sanctions targeting Iran-linked trade networks. If KIP's Dubai entities are identifiable as Iranian-linked: SDN designation risk. Even without formal designation, banks may de-risk proactively. OFAC is watching Iran-UAE financial flows closely during conflict.
impactKIP_fa: هاب دبی: عملیاتاً مرده. کریدور ایران-امارات از روز ۱ غیرفعال. فضای هوایی بسته. ابوظبی حمله (۲ کشته D27). ۲,۱۰۰+ پرتاب. سپاه: پایگاه‌های امارات 'هدف مشروع.'
بانکداری: خطر انجماد شدید. بانک‌های امارات حساب‌های ایرانی زیر نظر. بدون اخطار ممکن است مسدود شوند. اگر وجوه در سیستم بانکی امارات: برداشت فوری.
هرمز: انسداد فیزیکی. مین تأیید شده. عبور مطمئن = نفتکش نه کالای تجاری. واردصادرات KIP نیاز به هماهنگی اختصاصی.
پرسنل: ۱۱ کشته امارات. ۲,۱۰۰+ پرتاب. جابجایی موقت به عمان توصیه.
کالای انبار/ترانزیت: جبل‌علی و انبارهای دبی = گیرافتاده. ۲,۰۰۰ کشتی. بیمه ۵-۱۰٪.
درآمد: نزدیک صفر. هیچ کالایی جریان ندارد. ایران اینترنت ندارد. دبی لجستیک و بانک مشکل. زمان‌بندی: ماه‌ها، شاید ۱۲-۱۸ ماه.
تحریم: تحریم‌های ثانویه ممکن. اگر نهاد دبی ایرانی شناسایی شود: SDN. بانک‌ها پیش‌دستانه قطع.
prepKIP: IMMEDIATE — BANKING: If KIP has funds in UAE banks: assess withdrawal NOW. Contact bank relationship manager about compliance status. Ask directly: 'Are accounts with Iranian beneficial ownership under review?' If any ambiguity: move funds to a non-Gulf jurisdiction (Turkey, Georgia, Malaysia) BEFORE a potential freeze. Once frozen, recovery takes months/years even if legitimate.

IMMEDIATE — PERSONNEL: Any staff in Dubai/Abu Dhabi should temporarily relocate to Muscat (Oman), Tbilisi (Georgia), or Istanbul. UAE is receiving 70-80+ projectiles/day. Abu Dhabi debris killed 2 people D27. This is not theoretical risk — it is active combat fallout in a civilian area.

THIS WEEK — ALTERNATIVE HUB ASSESSMENT: Dubai is dead as a logistics hub for the foreseeable future. Evaluate alternatives: (1) Muscat, Oman — closest, but Oman ports also hit by IRGC drones, status 'compromised.' (2) Istanbul, Turkey — established Iran-Turkey trade corridor, but Turkey-Iran land border currently shut + NATO airspace restrictions. (3) Tbilisi, Georgia — emerging Iran trade route via Armenia, small but growing. (4) Kuala Lumpur — Malaysia has historically facilitated Iran trade, distant but functional.

THIS WEEK — INVENTORY AUDIT: Catalog everything stranded: goods in Jebel Ali, goods on vessels in Hormuz region, goods in Iranian warehouses (if contactable). Determine: what is insured, what can be claimed as force majeure, what can be rerouted if a corridor opens. Contact shipping lines about stranded cargo status.

FRIDAY MAR 28 — ESCALATION WATCH: If power plants struck: (1) Iran-side operations become impossible for extended period, (2) oil spikes above $120 = shipping costs skyrocket further, (3) humanitarian crisis deepens = international sanctions landscape may shift. If a deal emerges: (1) Hormuz reopening begins (weeks to months for mine clearance), (2) Dubai corridor still needs UAE airspace/banking normalization, (3) earliest partial resumption: 2-3 months post-ceasefire.

MEDIUM-TERM — DIVERSIFICATION: The war has exposed a fatal dependency: 100% of KIP's logistics flow through a single chokepoint (Hormuz) and a single hub (Dubai). Post-war, diversify: maintain Dubai but add a second hub outside the Gulf (Turkey or Georgia). Establish banking relationships in 2-3 jurisdictions. Never again have all trade finance in one country that's within missile range of Iran.

MEDIUM-TERM — INSURANCE: Current hull insurance 5-10% (from 0.15-0.25%). This makes most shipments uneconomical. Monitor: when premiums begin to drop = market signal that risk is decreasing. If safe passage framework expands to commercial cargo: premiums may drop for specific routes. Get quotes from multiple marine insurers NOW for when corridor reopens — pricing will be competitive early.
prepKIP_fa: فوری — بانکداری: وجوه امارات: ارزیابی برداشت فوری. بانک تماس. اگر ابهام: انتقال خارج خلیج (ترکیه، گرجستان، مالزی). مسدودی = ماه‌ها/سال‌ها.
فوری — پرسنل: دبی/ابوظبی → مسقط یا تفلیس یا استانبول. ۷۰-۸۰+ پرتاب/روز. ابوظبی ۲ کشته D27. ریسک فعال.
این هفته — هاب جایگزین: دبی مرده. ارزیابی: (۱) مسقط — نزدیک اما حمله شده. (۲) استانبول — مرز بسته اما ظرفیت. (۳) تفلیس — مسیر ارمنستان. (۴) کوالالامپور — تاریخی.
این هفته — موجودی: کالاهای جبل‌علی، کشتی‌ها، انبار ایران. بیمه، فورس ماژور، مسیر جایگزین. خطوط کشتیرانی تماس.
جمعه ۲۸: نیروگاه: ایران غیرممکن + نفت >$۱۲۰ + تحریم‌ها. معامله: هرمز هفته‌ها/ماه‌ها + دبی عادی‌سازی. اولین بازگشایی جزئی: ۲-۳ ماه.
میان‌مدت — تنوع: وابستگی ۱۰۰٪ به هرمز + دبی = آسیب‌پذیری کشنده. بعد جنگ: هاب دوم خارج خلیج. بانک ۲-۳ کشور.
میان‌مدت — بیمه: ۵-۱۰٪ = غیراقتصادی. نظارت: کاهش حق‌بیمه = سیگنال. اگر عبور تجاری = کاهش مسیر اختصاصی. استعلام از چند بیمه‌گر.
signalsKIP: GATE 1 — UAE BANKING: Any announcement from UAE Central Bank regarding Iran-linked accounts. Any OFAC SDN additions targeting UAE-based Iranian trade entities. Any reports of account freezes for Iranian businesses in Dubai. If you hear of ONE freeze in your network: assume yours is next and act immediately.

GATE 2 — HORMUZ REOPENING: Safe passage framework expanding but currently oil-only. Watch for: (1) commercial cargo inclusion, (2) mine clearance operations beginning, (3) insurance premium drops, (4) Tangsiri's successor appointment and posture. The RATE of expansion determines recovery timeline.

GATE 3 — FRIDAY DEADLINE: Power plant strikes = catastrophic for Iran-side operations. Oil spike = shipping costs surge. But also: if a deal emerges, Hormuz reopening begins. Either way, Friday fundamentally changes the trajectory. Watch real-time.

DUBAI OPERATIONS: UAE airspace closures (frequency and duration), Abu Dhabi/Dubai airport operational status, Jebel Ali port status, any reports of free zone evacuations or closures. Dubai DXB and Al Maktoum airports have been intermittently closed.

PERSONNEL SAFETY: UAE casualty reports, projectile interception rates, civil defense warnings. National death toll 11 and rising. IRGC considers UAE bases legitimate targets — civilian areas adjacent to military installations are highest risk.

ALTERNATIVE HUBS: Turkey-Iran border status (currently shut), Armenia-Iran border (open per US Embassy Yerevan), Georgia ports (Poti/Batumi capacity), Oman ports (compromised — IRGC drone hits). Any announcement of border reopenings = route opportunity.

INSURANCE MARKET: Marine insurance premium changes for Gulf routes. Current 5-10% hull is prohibitive. Watch Lloyd's of London and P&I club announcements. Premium drops = leading indicator of normalized shipping.

HOUTHI: Declared entry but no confirmed attacks. If activated: Bab al-Mandab closes = Red Sea route also blocked. KIP would lose BOTH Gulf AND Red Sea shipping options. Maintain zero Red Sea exposure until Houthi status clarifies.
signalsKIP_fa: دروازه ۱ — بانک امارات: بانک مرکزی + OFAC SDN + انجماد حساب‌ها. اگر یکی مسدود: فرض کنید بعدی شماست.
دروازه ۲ — بازگشایی هرمز: عبور فعلاً نفتی. نظارت: کالای تجاری، مین‌روبی، کاهش بیمه، جانشین تانگسیری.
دروازه ۳ — جمعه: نیروگاه = فاجعه ایران. نفت = افزایش هزینه. معامله = بازگشایی. هر دو مسیر تغییر اساسی.
عملیات دبی: فضای هوایی، فرودگاه، جبل‌علی، منطقه آزاد. بسته شدن‌های متناوب.
پرسنل: گزارش تلفات امارات. رهگیری پرتاب. مناطق نظامی مجاور = بالاترین ریسک.
هاب‌های جایگزین: ترکیه-ایران مرز (بسته)، ارمنستان (باز)، گرجستان (ظرفیت)، عمان (تهدید). بازگشایی = فرصت.
بیمه: لویدز و P&I. ۵-۱۰٪ غیراقتصادی. کاهش = شاخص پیشرو.
حوثی: اعلام بدون حمله. اگر فعال = باب‌المندب + هرمز. دسترسی دریایی صفر.

casualties.detail: Hengaw (D25): 6,530 killed incl 640 civilians. IRNA (state): 1,750+ killed (up from 1,500+). HRANA: 1,455 civilian + 217 children + 1,167 military + 669 unclassified = 3,291+ tracked. 18,551 injured. 82,000+ structures destroyed. 94 ambulances hit. Isfahan struck extensively D27.

Health Ministry: 1,094 killed (up from 1,072), including 121 children. ~1M displaced. IDF soldier killed (Staff Sgt. Greenberg, Golani). IDF speeding up Litani ops. Bridges destroyed. Beirut suburbs struck. Iran's 5th condition demands comprehensive end ALL fronts incl Lebanon.

18+ killed including 13 US military KIA. D27: 6 missile salvos — Bnei Brak (2 injured), Kfar Qassem, Tel Aviv (3 wounded, possible cluster munition), Hezbollah drone Galilee (2 hurt). 8,000 tons arms delivered via 200+ aircraft/ships.
96 killed in strikes. US embassy Baghdad: Americans leave immediately. PMF positions struck. US bases in Iraq region targeted.

UAE: 11 dead (debris killed 2 Abu Dhabi D27). UAE received 2,100+ missiles/drones total. Saudi: 32 drones + 1 ballistic targeting Ras Tanura/Ghawar/Abqaiq. Kuwait: airport fuel tank fire. Bahrain: 1 Moroccan killed. GCC 6-country joint condemnation.

Iran casualty figures vary 3-4x between sources. IRNA (state media): 1,750+. HRANA (human rights): 3,291+ tracked. Hengaw (Kurdish org, D25 report): 6,530. Fog of war + internet blackout (600+ hours) makes independent verification impossible. All figures should be treated as estimates.

casualties_fa.detail: هنگاو: ۶,۵۳۰ شامل ۶۴۰ غیرنظامی. IRNA: ۱,۷۵۰+. HRANA: ۳,۲۹۱+ ردیابی. ۱۸,۵۵۱ زخمی. ۸۲,۰۰۰+ سازه. ۹۴ آمبولانس. اصفهان.
۱,۰۹۴ شامل ۱۲۱ کودک. ~۱ میلیون آواره. سرباز اسرائیلی کشته. عملیات لیطانی. بیروت.
۱۸+ شامل ۱۳ نظامی آمریکا. ۶ سالوو — بنی‌براک + کفرقاسم + تل‌آویو. ۸,۰۰۰ تن تسلیحات.
۹۶ کشته. سفارت بغداد: آمریکایی‌ها خروج. PMF.
امارات ۱۱. عربستان ۳۲ پهپاد + ۱ بالستیک. کویت فرودگاه. بحرین مراکشی. GCC محکومیت.
IRNA: ۱,۷۵۰+. HRANA: ۳,۲۹۱+. هنگاو: ۶,۵۳۰. اینترنت ۶۰۰+ ساعت = تأیید مستقل غیرممکن.

humanitarian: Internet blackout: Day 27 — 600+ HOURS total. LONGEST IN ANY COUNTRY'S HISTORY (NetBlocks). Amnesty: 'deliberate.' $35.7M/day economic loss. Total communication isolation for 90M people.
Displacement: Lebanon ~1M displaced. Iran unknown (no communications). UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain partial evacuations. 2,000 vessels / 20,000 seafarers stranded in Hormuz region.

Infrastructure: 82,000+ structures destroyed in Iran. 94 ambulances hit. Hospitals operating at 1/3 capacity. Food prices tripled. Power grid INTACT but Friday deadline threatens 90M without electricity.
Nuclear safety: IAEA locked out. Isfahan/Natanz/Fordow under strike. 440kg enriched uranium (~11 weapons worth). Israel speeding up arms factory targeting. Environmental contamination risk.
Medical crisis: HRANA: 18,551 injured in Iran. 217 children killed. Hospitals overwhelmed. Medical supply chains severed. Internet blackout prevents coordination.

Economic: Oil $106 (+3.8%). Insurance 5-10% hull (from 0.15-0.25%). Global shipping disrupted. 2,000 vessels stranded. Iran toll legislation. Philippines declared national emergency. Japan 30-day reserves.
humanitarian_fa: اینترنت: ۲۷ = ۶۰۰+ ساعت. طولانی‌ترین تاریخ هر کشور. عفو بین‌الملل: 'عمدی.' $۳۵.۷M/روز. انزوای ۹۰ میلیون.
آوارگی: لبنان ~۱M. ایران نامشخص. خلیج تخلیه بخشی. ۲,۰۰۰ کشتی / ۲۰,۰۰۰ دریانورد.
زیرساخت: ۸۲,۰۰۰+ سازه. ۹۴ آمبولانس. بیمارستان ۱/۳. غذا سه‌برابر. برق سالم اما جمعه.
هسته‌ای: IAEA قفل. ۴۴۰kg. اسرائیل سرعت تسلیحاتی. آلودگی محیطی.
پزشکی: ۱۸,۵۵۱ زخمی. ۲۱۷ کودک کشته. بیمارستان. اینترنت = هماهنگی ندارد.
اقتصادی: نفت $۱۰۶. بیمه ۵-۱۰٪. ۲,۰۰۰ کشتی. فیلیپین اضطراری. ژاپن ۳۰ روز.
diplomacy: 15-Point Peace Plan (D26): NYT: Pakistan delivered US plan demanding nuclear dismantlement (Natanz/Isfahan/Fordow), enrichment stop, IAEA transfer, missile suspension, proxy ceasefire, Hormuz reopening. Offers sanctions relief + civilian nuclear + snapback removal. 1-month ceasefire proposed.

Iran FORMALLY REJECTED (D27): FM Araghchi: 'no negotiations, war ends on our terms.' 5 counter-conditions: (1) end aggression, (2) guarantees no recurrence, (3) reparations, (4) comprehensive end ALL fronts incl Lebanon, (5) sovereignty over Hormuz. US: conditions 'ridiculous and unrealistic' (WSJ).

Trump 5-day postponement (D23): Postponed power plant strikes citing 'productive talks.' Iran denied direct talks but CBS: FM confirmed 'received points through mediators.' Deadline: Friday Mar 28 = 2 days remaining.

Intermediaries: Pakistan (primary — delivered plan, first Hormuz transit). Oman (traditional channel). Qatar. China (tacit support via safe passage). India (bilateral Hormuz deal). No direct US-Iran channel.

Israel position: Ambassador Danon: Israel NOT part of US-Iran talks. IDF speeding up independently. Katz: 'increase significantly.' IDF wants 'several more weeks.' Creating fait accompli. US-Israel divergence deepest since D1.

G7 FM meeting (Friday): Rubio in France. Coincides with deadline. Could produce multilateral framework. GCC 6-country condemnation. Germany: 'catastrophe for world's economies.' International pressure building but no enforcement mechanism.
diplomacy_fa: طرح ۱۵ بندی (D26): NYT: پاکستان تحویل. خلع سلاح هسته‌ای + IAEA + موشک + پروکسی + هرمز. تحریم‌ها + هسته‌ای غیرنظامی. آتش‌بس ۱ ماهه.
ایران رسماً رد (D27): عراقچی: 'مذاکره نه.' ۵ شرط: توقف تجاوز، تضمین، غرامت، همه جبهه‌ها، حاکمیت هرمز. آمریکا: 'مسخره.'
ترامپ تعویق ۵ روزه (D23): نیروگاه تعویق. مهلت جمعه ۲۸ = ۲ روز.
میانجی‌ها: پاکستان (اصلی). عمان. قطر. چین. هند. مستقیم ندارد.
اسرائیل: دانون: طرف نیست. سرعت. واگرایی عمیق‌ترین.
G7 جمعه: روبیو. همزمان مهلت. GCC ۶ کشور. آلمان 'فاجعه.' فشار اما مکانیزم ندارد.
publicOpinion: Pew Research (D27): 59% Americans say WRONG decision to attack Iran. 61% disapprove Trump handling. 54% expect 6+ months. 88% Democrats oppose. 71% Republicans support. UNPRECEDENTED for 27-day-old conflict — Iraq War took months to reach similar disapproval.
AP-NORC (D27): 59% say military action has gone too far. Cross-validates Pew findings.

Republican coalition fracture: Republican-leaning independents split 52-45 = Trump's coalition fraying on Iran. This is the critical political indicator — if it drops below 50: war becomes politically unsustainable.
Congressional: House Armed Services Committee 'unsatisfied' with administration briefing. Bipartisan pressure building. No AUMF vote yet.

International: GCC 6-country joint condemnation. Germany defense minister: 'catastrophe for world's economies.' G7 FM meeting Friday. Philippines national emergency. Japan 30-day reserves. Global consensus against continuation.
publicOpinion_fa: پیو (D27): ۵۹٪ اشتباه. ۶۱٪ عدم تأیید. ۵۴٪ ۶+ ماه. ۸۸٪ دموکرات مخالف. ۷۱٪ جمهوری‌خواه. بی‌سابقه.
جمهوری‌خواهان: مستقل ۵۲-۴۵ شکاف. اگر زیر ۵۰: غیرقابل دوام سیاسی.
بین‌المللی: GCC ۶ کشور. آلمان 'فاجعه.' G7 جمعه. فیلیپین اضطراری. ژاپن ۳۰ روز.
hormuz: Blockade status: Strait of Hormuz physically blocked by confirmed Iranian mines (Maham 3/7). IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri — architect of blockade — killed D27 in Bandar Abbas strike. Naval command succession uncertain. 21% of global oil transits Hormuz.

Safe passage framework: Iran established bilateral safe passage via UN letter (D26). Non-hostile ships transit with coordination. Expanding: Pakistan (first Aframax), India ('open' — 2 LPG tankers), Thailand (tanker), China (priority). Explicitly EXCLUDES US-flagged/linked vessels.

Mine threat: Physical mines confirmed. Clearance estimated weeks to months even without opposition. Tangsiri death may affect mine-laying coordination but mines already deployed. US/coalition mine countermeasures would require Hormuz entry.

Toll legislation: Iran preparing Hormuz toll legislation (Fars News). Would formalize Iran's claim of sovereignty over strait. Bureaucratic momentum suggests institutional policy, not personal to Tangsiri.
Insurance crisis: Premiums surged to 5-10% of hull value (from 0.15-0.25% pre-war). 2,000 vessels / 20,000 seafarers stranded. Even safe passage ships face elevated insurance costs.

82nd Airborne: 2,000-3,000 paratroopers deploying. Maj Gen Tegtmeier HQ. Kharg Island scenario. Combined with 2 MEUs = 6-8K ground troops. 50K total US forces in region. If activated for Hormuz: total war scenario.

Key question: Does Hormuz blockade survive its architect? Evidence suggests YES — safe passage expanded SAME DAY as Tangsiri death reported. Framework is institutional, not personal. But successor's posture will determine whether blockade tightens or loosens.
hormuz_fa: انسداد: مین تأیید شده (ماهام ۳/۷). تانگسیری (معمار) کشته. جانشینی نامشخص. ۲۱٪ نفت جهان.
عبور مطمئن: نامه سازمان ملل. پاکستان + هند + تایلند + چین. آمریکا خارج.
مین: هفته‌ها تا ماه‌ها پاکسازی. تانگسیری مرده اما مین‌ها مستقر.
هوابرد ۸۲: ۲-۳ هزار. خارگ. ۶-۸ هزار زمینی. ۵۰ هزار منطقه. اگر فعال = جنگ تمام‌عیار.
سؤال: انسداد بدون تانگسیری؟ شواهد = بله. عبور همان روز = نهادی. جانشین تعیین‌کننده.
keyActors: United States: Primary belligerent. 50K+ troops in region. 82nd Airborne deploying. 8,000 tons arms to Israel. Trump seeking deal (Pew 59% pressure). Friday deadline for power plants. Diverging from Israel on timeline.

Israel: Operating independently. IDF speeding up strikes (Isfahan arms factories). Ambassador Danon: NOT part of US-Iran talks. Wants 'several more weeks.' Creating fait accompli. US-Israel divergence deepest since D1.

Iran (IRGC): Still launching 6+ salvos/day at Israel. Tangsiri (Navy Commander) killed D27. Formally rejected 15-point plan. 5 counter-conditions. Safe passage framework expanding. Mojtaba absent Day 18. Toll legislation.
Pakistan: Primary mediator. Delivered 15-point plan. First non-Iranian Hormuz transit (Aframax). Bridge between US and Iran.
India: Declared Hormuz 'open.' 2 LPG tankers transited. 3 tankers released by Iran. Bilateral safe passage deal. Balancing US relations with energy needs.
Hezbollah/Lebanon: Active front. 1,094+ killed. Drone from Galilee downed. IDF Litani operations expanding. Iran's 5th condition: comprehensive end ALL fronts.
Houthis (Yemen): Declared entry but NO confirmed attacks yet. If activated: BOTH chokepoints (Hormuz + Bab al-Mandab). Biggest wild card.
GCC (UAE/Saudi/Kuwait/Bahrain/Qatar/Jordan): 6-country joint condemnation. UAE: 11 dead, 2,100+ missiles/drones received. Saudi: Ras Tanura/Ghawar/Abqaiq targeted. Demanding ceasefire.
82nd Airborne: 2-3K paratroopers. Maj Gen Tegtmeier. Kharg Island contingency. Combined with 2 MEUs = 6-8K ground troops. Escalation trigger.
IAEA: Locked out of Iran nuclear sites. 440kg enriched uranium. Environmental verification impossible. Nuclear dimension unresolved regardless of ceasefire.
keyActors_fa: آمریکا: ۵۰ هزار+. هوابرد ۸۲. ۸,۰۰۰ تن تسلیحات اسرائیل. ترامپ معامله (پیو ۵۹٪). جمعه نیروگاه. واگرایی اسرائیل.
اسرائیل: مستقل. اصفهان تسلیحاتی سرعت. دانون: طرف نیست. 'هفته‌ها.' عمل انجام‌شده.
ایران (سپاه): ۶+ سالوو/روز. تانگسیری کشته. رد ۱۵ بند. ۵ ضدشرط. عبور گسترش. مجتبی ۱۸. عوارض.
پاکستان: میانجی اصلی. طرح تحویل. اولین عبور غیرایرانی.
حوثی: اعلام بدون حمله. هر دو تنگه. بزرگ‌ترین ناشناخته.
GCC: ۶ کشور محکومیت. امارات ۱۱. عربستان هدف. آتش‌بس خواهان.
هوابرد ۸۲: ۲-۳ هزار. خارگ. ۶-۸ هزار زمینی. محرک تشدید.
